description: Schema fragment (menu_interface.json). Merged into src/schema.json by src/schema/build.py. Do not load directly -- cross-fragment $ref uses '#/definitions/...' against the bundled output.

menu_interface.description: /interface -- top-level interface menu. Houses physical Ethernet ports, bridges, VLAN sub-interfaces, Wi-Fi radios, tunnels, and the `list` taxonomy. Known sub-menus get typed validation; unknown ones fall back to `menu_generic`.

menu_interface_list.description: /interface/list -- named interface lists (e.g. `mgmt`, `wan`, `lan`) used by firewall rules, ND, MAC-server, etc. Accepts either a plain list of list rows, or an object with `_items` + a `member` sub-list for membership rows.

member.description: /interface/list/member -- membership rows mapping interfaces into a list.

menu_interface_bridge.description: /interface/bridge -- bridge devices. Either a plain list of bridge rows, or an object with `_items` plus `port` and `vlan` sub-lists for port attachments and bridge-VLAN entries.

port.description: /interface/bridge/port -- attaches an interface to a bridge.

menu_interface_wifi.description: /interface/wifi -- Wi-Fi radios and virtual APs (wifi-qcom driver). Either a plain list of wifi-interface rows, or an object with `_items` plus profile sub-menus (`datapath`, `security`, `channel`, `configuration`, `aaa`, `steering`, ...).

configuration.description: /interface/wifi/configuration -- binds datapath + security + channel into a named profile referenced by `/interface/wifi`.

menu_array_item_interface_vlan.description: /interface/vlan -- 802.1Q VLAN sub-interfaces (L3 termination). Either a plain list of VLAN rows, or an object with `_items` for the rows + a sibling `description`.

menu_array_item_interface_ethernet.description: /interface/ethernet -- physical Ethernet ports. Almost always tuned via `set [find default-name=etherN]`. Either a plain list of rows, or an object with `_items` + a sibling `description`.

item_interface_list.description: /interface/list -- a named interface list (membership rows live under .member).

operation.description: `add` creates a new list; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.list.mgmt`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"management ifaces"`.

name.description: List name (token-style; referenced from filters and member rows). Example: `iac.list.mgmt`.

include.description: Comma list of nested lists to union into this list. Example: `iac.list.lan,iac.list.iot`.

exclude.description: Comma list of nested lists to exclude from this list. Example: `iac.list.guest`.

item_interface_list_member.description: /interface/list/member -- an interface placed into a list.

operation.description: `add` enrolls a new member; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find list=mgmt interface=ether1]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.list.mgmt.ether1`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"uplink to office"`.

disabled.description: If true, the membership is inactive. Example: `false`.

operation.description: Physical ports are pre-created -- prefer `set`. Example: `set`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.ether.wan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"ISP uplink"`.

name.description: New logical name of the port. Example: `iac.ether.wan`.

default-name.description: Factory port label used for `set [find default-name=...]`. Example: `ether1`.

l2mtu.description: L2 MTU in bytes (for jumbo/Q-in-Q). Example: `1592`.

arp.description: ARP behaviour on the port. Example: `enabled`.

speed.description: Forced port speed when auto-negotiation is off. Example: `1Gbps`.

full-duplex.description: Forced duplex when auto-negotiation is off. Example: `true`.

advertise.description: Comma list of speeds/duplex advertised in auto-neg. Example: `1000M-full,100M-full`.

mac-address.description: Override the port's MAC address. Example: `02:11:22:33:44:55`.

poe-out.description: PoE-out mode on supported ports. Example: `off`.

disabled.description: If true, the port is administratively down. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` creates a new bridge; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.bridge.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN bridge"`.

pvid.description: Default port VLAN id for untagged ingress on the bridge itself. Example: `1`.

ether-type.description: Tag ether-type for VLAN frames. Example: `0x8100`.

frame-types.description: Default accepted frame types on the bridge. Example: `admit-all`.

ingress-filtering.description: Drop tagged frames whose VLAN is not on the port. Example: `true`.

mtu.description: L3 MTU; `auto` matches smallest port. Example: `auto`.

admin-mac.description: Static MAC for the bridge interface. Example: `02:CA:FE:00:00:01`.

auto-mac.description: Auto-pick the bridge MAC from a member port. Example: `false`.

fast-forward.description: Enable bridge fast-forwarding for 2-port bridges. Example: `false`.

igmp-snooping.description: Enable IGMP/MLD snooping on the bridge. Example: `false`.

dhcp-snooping.description: Enable DHCP snooping (requires bridge filters). Example: `false`.

disabled.description: If true, the bridge is administratively down. Example: `false`.

item_interface_bridge_port.description: /interface/bridge/port -- attaches an interface to a bridge.

operation.description: `add` attaches a port; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find bridge=bridge-lan interface=ether2]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.bridge.lan.port.ether2`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"trunk to switch"`.

pvid.description: Native (untagged) VLAN id assigned to ingress frames. Example: `100`.

ingress-filtering.description: Drop tagged frames whose VLAN is not on the port. Example: `true`.

tag-stacking.description: Push an outer tag on ingress (Q-in-Q). Example: `false`.

horizon.description: Split-horizon group: ports with same horizon don't forward to each other. Example: `1`.

hw.description: Use hardware-offloaded switching for this port. Example: `true`.

multicast-router.description: Multicast-router role for IGMP snooping. Example: `temporary-query`.

broadcast-flood.description: Forward broadcasts to this port. Example: `true`.

multicast-flood.description: Forward unknown multicast to this port. Example: `true`.

unknown-unicast-flood.description: Forward unknown unicast to this port. Example: `true`.

unknown-multicast-flood.description: Forward unknown multicast to this port. Example: `true`.

auto-isolate.description: Block port until BPDU is seen on an edge port. Example: `false`.

bpdu-guard.description: Disable port if any BPDU is received. Example: `false`.

disabled.description: If true, the port row is administratively disabled. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` creates a new VLAN row; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

filter.description: Find expression for `set`. Example: `[find bridge=bridge-lan vlan-ids=100]`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.bridge.lan.vlan.100`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"office VLAN"`.

tagged.description: Comma list of ports carrying these VLANs tagged. Example: `bridge-lan,ether1`.

untagged.description: Comma list of ports carrying these VLANs untagged. Example: `ether2,ether3`.

current-tagged.description: Read-only effective tagged set; not usually set in IaC. Example: omit.

current-untagged.description: Read-only effective untagged set; not usually set in IaC. Example: omit.

mvrp-forbidden.description: Ports forbidden to dynamically register the VLAN via MVRP. Example: omit.

disabled.description: If true, the VLAN row is disabled. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` creates a new VLAN interface; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.vlan.lan100`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"office VLAN"`.

use-service-tag.description: Use S-VLAN (0x88a8) ether-type instead of C-VLAN. Example: `false`.

loop-protect-disable-time.description: How long to keep the iface disabled after a loop. Example: `5m`.

mvrp.description: Enable Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol on this iface. Example: `false`.

mtu.description: L3 MTU on the VLAN. Example: `1500`.

arp.description: ARP behaviour on the VLAN. Example: `enabled`.

mac-address.description: Override the VLAN iface MAC. Example: `02:11:22:33:44:55`.

disabled.description: If true, the VLAN iface is administratively down. Example: `false`.

item_interface_wifi.description: /interface/wifi -- a Wi-Fi radio or virtual AP (wifi-qcom).

operation.description: Physical radios exist by default -- prefer `set`; `add` registers a virtual AP. Example: `set`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.wifi.wifi1`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"5GHz AP"`.

name.description: New logical name of the wifi iface. Example: `iac.wifi.wifi1`.

default-name.description: Factory radio label used for `set [find default-name=...]`. Example: `wifi1`.

master-interface.description: Parent radio for a virtual AP (when adding a multi-SSID). Example: `iac.wifi.wifi1`.

channel.description: Named channel profile to apply. Example: `iac.wifi.ch.5ghz`.

security.description: Named security profile to apply. Example: `iac.wifi.sec.wpa3`.

datapath.description: Named datapath profile to apply. Example: `iac.wifi.dp.lan`.

aaa.description: Named AAA profile to apply. Example: `iac.wifi.aaa.radius`.

steering.description: Named steering profile to apply. Example: `iac.wifi.steer.default`.

mtu.description: L3 MTU on the wifi iface. Example: `1500`.

l2mtu.description: L2 MTU on the wifi iface. Example: `1600`.

arp.description: ARP behaviour on the wifi iface. Example: `enabled`.

disabled.description: If true, the radio/AP is administratively down. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` registers a new profile; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.wifi.dp.lan`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"LAN datapath"`.

bridge.description: Bridge clients are placed into. Example: `iac.bridge.lan`.

vlan-id.description: VLAN tagged on traffic from this datapath. Example: `100`.

interface-list.description: Interface-list to enrol the wifi iface into. Example: `iac.list.lan`.

client-isolation.description: Block client-to-client traffic via the AP. Example: `true`.

client-to-client-forwarding.description: Inverse of client isolation -- allow client-to-client. Example: `false`.

arp.description: ARP behaviour on associated clients. Example: `enabled`.

openflow-switch.description: Attach datapath traffic to an OpenFlow switch. Example: omit.

disabled.description: If true, the profile is disabled. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` registers a new profile; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.wifi.sec.wpa3`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"home WPA3"`.

authentication-types.description: Comma list of acceptable auth modes. Example: `wpa2-psk,wpa3-psk`.

encryption.description: Comma list of pairwise ciphers. Example: `ccmp,gcmp`.

group-encryption.description: Comma list of group ciphers. Example: `ccmp`.

passphrase.description: PSK passphrase; prefer `$NAME` secret reference. Example: `$WIFI_PASSPHRASE`.

owe-transition-interface.description: Companion OWE-transition iface name. Example: omit.

ft-over-ds.description: Allow FT over the distribution system. Example: `false`.

ft-nas-identifier.description: NAS identifier used in FT. Example: `ap-01`.

ft-preserve-vlanid.description: Keep VLAN id during FT roam. Example: `true`.

disable-pmkid.description: Strip the PMKID from EAPOL frames (KRACK mitigation). Example: `false`.

eap-username.description: Outer identity for station-mode EAP. Example: `client01`.

eap-password.description: Password for station-mode EAP; prefer `$NAME` secret reference. Example: `$EAP_PASSWORD`.

eap-tls-mode.description: How TLS certificates are validated. Example: `verify-certificate`.

client-certificate.description: Client cert name used by EAP-TLS. Example: `client-cert`.

trust-certificate.description: CA cert trusted to validate the server. Example: `corp-ca`.

disabled.description: If true, the profile is disabled. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` registers a new profile; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.wifi.ch.5ghz`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"5GHz outdoor"`.

frequency.description: Center frequency or comma list to scan. Example: `5180`.

reselect-interval.description: How often to revisit channel choice. Example: `1h`.

disabled.description: If true, the profile is disabled. Example: `false`.

operation.description: `add` registers a new profile; `set` updates one matched by `filter`. Example: `add`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.wifi.cfg.home`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"home AP"`.

antenna-gain.description: External antenna gain in dBi. Example: `0`.

tx-chains.description: Comma list of TX chain indices. Example: `0,1`.

rx-chains.description: Comma list of RX chain indices. Example: `0,1`.

manager.description: CAPsMAN manager identity (for CAP mode). Example: omit.

disabled.description: If true, the profile is disabled. Example: `false`.
